title: Cyclosorus pauciflorus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus pauciflorus is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as the marsh fern family. This plant is a species of fern, belonging to the division Pteridophyta. Further classification places it within the genus Cyclosorus, a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species is also known as the few-flowered cyclosorus fern due to its unique reproductive characteristics. At the highest taxonomic level, it is classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cyclosorus pauciflorus is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Its leaves and stems are used in local remedies to alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Additionally, the plant is believed to possess antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of infections. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and potential side effects.

These constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
